zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane de la Zouche於大約1475年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯時，她的父親Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur17歲，母親Joan Dinham33歲。她於大約1500年與Sir Edward Hungerford結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1518年7月之前在英國維爾爾海斯波利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane de la Zouche于大约1475年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾哈利沃斯时，她的父亲Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur17岁，母亲Joan Dinham33岁。她于大约1500年与Sir Edward Hungerford结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1518年7月之前在英国維爾爾海斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

variants.name: Zaucha
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Casimir, Franciszek, Kazimierz, Krzysztof, Mieczyslaw, Rafal, Wojciech.
Polish: descriptive nickname from za uchem ‘behind the ear’, denoting someone who had something behind one of his ears. Compare Zoucha .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane de la Zouche was born about 1475, in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, her father, Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur, was 17 and her mother, Joan Dinham, was 33. She married Sir Edward Hungerford about 1500. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died before July 1518, in Heytesbury, Wiltshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane de la Zouche est née environ 1475, à Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Joan Dinham, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward Hungerford environ 1500. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée avant juillet 1518, à Heytesbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane de la Zouche um 1475 in Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur 17 und ihre Mutter Joan Dinham 33. Sie hat um 1500 Sir Edward Hungerford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb vor Juli 1518 in Heytesbury, Wiltshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de la Zouche è nata il circa 1475 nel Harringworth, Northamptonshire, England, sua padre, Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Joan Dinham, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Sir Edward Hungerford circa 1500. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì prima del luglio 1518, nel Heytesbury, Wiltshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane de la Zoucheが約1475年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアハーリングウオルスで生まれた時，父Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maurは17歳，母Joan Dinhamは33歳であった。約1500年にSir Edward Hungerfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1518年7月以前にイギリスウィルトシュアイアヘイテスブリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane de la Zouche(이)가 대략 1475년 영국 노르섬프토실르 해링워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Dinham(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 대략 1500년 Sir Edward Hungerford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1518년 7월 이전 영국 윌츠히르 헤이츠버이에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane de la Zouche nasceu aproximadamente 1475, em Harringworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Joan Dinham, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Edward Hungerford aproximadamente 1500. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu antes julho de 1518, em Heytesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane de la Zouche родилась около 1475 г. в местности харрингворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de la Zouche 7th Baron Zouche 8th Baron St Maur было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Dinham, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward Hungerford около 1500 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла перед июля 1518 г. в местности хитсбури, уилцхир, Англия.

Se casó con Sir Edward Hungerford aproximadamente 1500. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió antes de julio de 1518, en Heytesbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra.
